_source.title: Utilization of effluent from nitrifying biofilter tank of recirculating Nile tilapia farm to cultivate microalgal Scenedesmus armatus
_source.abstract: This research cultivates microalgal Scenedesmus armatus using effluent containing high nitrate concentration from nitrifying biofilter tanks of commercial recirculating tilapia culture system to produce microalgal biomass and lutein. The 9-days batch cultivation of S. armatus in 1-L photobioreactors using nitrifying effluent was conducted and compared with the cultivation using the standard BG-11 medium under the same condition. The results revealed that the cultivation using nitrifying effluent had biomass productivity and lutein content of 71.71 mg/L.d and 1.12 mg/g, respectively, which were statistically insignificant difference (p 0.05) from those cultured with BG-11 medium. The obtained results implied that the concurrent production of microalgal biomass and high-valued chemicals were possible using low cost resource such as the effluent from recirculating aquaculture.

_source.title: Efficacy of adjuvanted Streptococcus agalactiae vaccine by montanide ISA 763 A VG in nile tilapia (Oreochromis niloticus Linn.)
_source.abstract: This research investigated the efficacy of adjuvant (Montanide ISA 763 A VG) at concentrations of 0, 5, 10, 20 and 50 percent in formalin-killed Streptococcus agalactiae vaccine in Nile tilapia (Oreochromis niloticus Linn.). The results showed that adjuvant at 20 and 50 percent induced significantly higher protection (survival and RPS value) than other groups after three months of experimental trial (P LT 0.05). However, only the antibody titer of 20 percent adjuvant was significantly higher than the control group (P LT 0.05). The second experiment aimed to find the efficacy of a single vaccination with 10 and 20 percent adjuvant compared with non-adjuvant vaccine with a booster dose. The results showed that after the booster vaccination without adjuvant, the antibody titer was significantly higher than other treatments (P LT 0.05). However, in terms of protection (survival and RPS), the vaccines with both 10 and 20 percent adjuvant showed similar efficacy to the booster vaccination until the third month of the trial (P GT 0.05), and were significantly different from the vaccine without adjuvant (non-booster group) and from the control (non-vaccination) (P LT 0.05). Thus, adjuvant vaccine was as effective as booster vaccination for the first three months of the four-month trial. Further study with the aim to extend protection with a single dose of adjuvant vaccine is being conducted
